evals.prompt: Help me launch the next version of this project on Product Hunt, Hacker News, Reddit, and wherever else makes sense.
evals.expected_output: Should use the launch command path. Should read GROWTH.md first or run init if it is missing. Should produce an indie/OSS launch plan rather than a SaaS funnel. Should verify pre-flight readiness before announcing: install artifact works, README is current, release notes exist, demo asset exists, and the project has a true hook. Should choose channels based on GROWTH.md rather than blindly using every platform. Should include launch copy drafts for the relevant channels and a day-of timeline. Should not ask for upvotes, fabricate traction, or recommend paid directory spam.
assertions: Routes to launch
Loads GROWTH.md or runs init first
Checks pre-flight readiness before channel planning
Chooses channels from project context
Includes honest hooks and channel-specific drafts
Avoids SaaS funnel language and lead capture
Does not fabricate numbers, users, endorsements, or upvote requests

evals.prompt: Rewrite the README intro, GitHub repo description, topics, and release notes so they sound sharper but not salesy.
evals.expected_output: Should use the copy command path. Should read GROWTH.md and the current README before writing. Should keep copy developer-to-developer, factual, and specific. Should provide repo description, topic suggestions, README intro, and release notes in appropriate structures. Should flag any claim that cannot be verified. Should avoid buzzwords, fake social proof, enterprise SaaS CTAs, and em dashes.
assertions: Routes to copy
Reads GROWTH.md and current README
Produces repo-native copy rather than generic marketing copy
Structures release notes with Added/Changed/Fixed when appropriate
Flags unverifiable claims
Avoids buzzwords, fake metrics, fake testimonials, and em dashes
Uses install/star/download language instead of book-demo or sales-funnel language

evals.prompt: We need launch visuals: screenshots, a demo GIF, Product Hunt gallery images, and an OG image.
evals.expected_output: Should use the visuals command path. Should read GROWTH.md for product, platform, brand, and channels. Should require real screenshots or screen recordings from the running project and explicitly reject AI-generated UI mockups or fake data. Should prioritize demo asset, primary screenshot, secondary screenshots, OG image, and optional icon polish. Should specify dimensions, capture settings, export formats, optimization, and where each asset will be used. Should hand off landing page visual craft to impeccable and DMG installer art to dmg-background if relevant.
assertions: Routes to visuals
Loads project context before asset planning
Requires real product screenshots or recordings
Rejects AI-generated UI and fake data
Provides a prioritized shot list mapped to surfaces
Includes dimensions and export/optimization guidance
Mentions handoff boundaries for impeccable or dmg-background when relevant

evals.prompt: Turn this release into posts for X, Mastodon, Reddit, and maybe a short demo clip.
evals.expected_output: Should use the social command path. Should read GROWTH.md channels and only choose platforms that fit the project. Should lead with a concrete demo asset and real release facts. Should draft 2-3 platform-specific posts with hooks, not a generic content calendar. Should include a short clip spec only if video makes sense. Should avoid LinkedIn carousel/TikTok trend-chasing by default, fake urgency, engagement bait, and fabricated milestones.
assertions: Routes to social
Uses channels from GROWTH.md
Leads with a real asset and real release facts
Produces platform-specific post drafts
Includes clip spec when appropriate
Avoids generic daily posting calendars
Avoids fake milestones, fake urgency, and engagement bait

evals.prompt: Position this project against the main alternatives and help me explain why someone would choose us.
evals.expected_output: Should use the position command path. Should read GROWTH.md differentiators and competitors. Should verify current competitor facts before publishing comparisons. Should acknowledge competitor strengths and state where each tool is best. Should produce a one-sentence position, ranked differentiators, honest comparison table, best-for routing, and user-language phrases. Should not smear competitors or claim features/pricing without verification.
assertions: Routes to position
Reads differentiators and competitors from GROWTH.md
Verifies current competitor facts before public comparison
Acknowledges competitor strengths honestly
Produces positioning brief and comparison table
Includes best-for routing by user type
Flags unverifiable competitor claims

evals.prompt: Plan a one-page site for this project with SEO, AI citation, FAQ schema, and a clear install CTA.
evals.expected_output: Should use the web command path. Should read GROWTH.md for product facts, pricing, differentiators, links, and install path. Should scope to a single landing page or README discoverability, not a multi-page SaaS site or blog engine. Should provide page structure, title tag, meta description, heading map, FAQ, and accurate JSON-LD SoftwareApplication/FAQPage schema. Should use the real install/download/star CTA, not lead capture. Should hand visual design polish to impeccable if needed.
assertions: Routes to web
Loads GROWTH.md before page planning
Keeps scope to one page or README discoverability
Includes title, meta, heading map, FAQ, and JSON-LD
Uses accurate schema values only
Uses install/download/star CTA instead of lead capture
Refers visual craft to impeccable when appropriate

evals.prompt: How do we grow the open-source community around this project and get more stars and contributors without being spammy?
evals.expected_output: Should use the community command path. Should read GROWTH.md channels. Should treat the repo as the community home, focusing on README, CONTRIBUTING.md, issue templates, good-first-issues, changelog, and fast maintainer responses. Should suggest honest star growth through useful releases, relevant awesome lists, and real community participation. Should avoid paid community programs, fake activity, star exchanges, ambassador swag programs, or SaaS community funnels.
assertions: Routes to community
Reads channels from GROWTH.md
Focuses on repo readiness and contributor pathways
Recommends good-first-issues, issue templates, CONTRIBUTING, and changelog
Suggests honest community engagement in relevant places
Rejects fake stars, engagement farming, and star exchanges
Avoids SaaS-style ambassador or community funnel machinery

evals.prompt: Make this project easier to promote. We don't have any growth docs yet.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as a general growth invocation that needs setup. Should check for GROWTH.md and run init if it is missing, empty, or placeholder. Should inspect the project to draft facts rather than inventing them. Should ask the user to confirm uncertain fields such as pricing model, differentiators, competitors, target channels, and voice. Should write or propose a GROWTH.md using the schema before doing downstream launch/copy/web/social work.
assertions: Runs setup before promotion work
Checks for GROWTH.md
Routes to init when GROWTH.md is missing or placeholder
Drafts from repository facts instead of guesses
Asks user to confirm uncertain fields
Uses the GROWTH.md schema
Does not proceed with public claims before context is confirmed
